summary:
The cornua section has a relatively lower risk of rupturing compared to other areas such as the ampullary portion because it is well-vascularized; this supports prolonged fetal life even though ultimately not viable. Based primarily on vasculature support, \(\boxed{\text{cornua}}\) remains optimal under these conditions regarding extended but still non-survivable gestation periods within an ectopic setting involving fallopian tubes specifically. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related directly towards answering posed query without extraneous details or tangential information,
